author: David Reed
summary: One change. chg-10 (P-5 / T-18): runtime evidence-grounding detector. A deterministic post-agent orchestrator check (before confidence routing) forces human review on any decision citing an evidence id absent from the submission -- the production-path GC-018/019 anti-hallucination gate. Paired system_prompt change (DecisionAgent v2.6->v2.7) makes the model populate cited_evidence_ids. Grounds against submission evidence ids only (RAG chunks carry no agent-visible ids).

changes.description: Add a deterministic evidence-grounding detector (agents/evidence_grounding.py) wired into the orchestrator after the decision, before confidence routing: any cited_evidence_id not resolving to a submission EvidenceItem forces IN_REVIEW + finding.ungrounded_evidence audit (new EscalationReason.UNGROUNDED_EVIDENCE). PAIRED (AHE rule): DecisionAgent prompt v2.6->v2.7 makes the model populate cited_evidence_ids with only ids present. New defaulted field AuthorizationDecision.cited_evidence_ids.
changes.failure_pattern: PACCA's anti-hallucination guarantee lived only at eval time (GC-018/019 golden cases via the LLM judge). In production nothing checked, per run, that the decision's cited evidence actually existed in the submission -- a decision resting on fabricated or misattributed evidence would flow straight through confidence routing.
changes.root_cause: The decision output carried a free-text rationale with no structured, checkable evidence citations, and there was no deterministic post-agent detector. Promoting the eval-time anti-hallucination gate to a runtime detector needs both: the model must cite by id (prompt change) AND code must verify the ids (orchestrator check) -- the paper's prescribed prompt+structure pairing.

evidence.note: Pure detector: unresolved_cited_evidence returns absent ids (deduped, ordered), [] when fully grounded/empty. Orchestrator: an ungrounded citation (id 'e99') forces IN_REVIEW + finding.ungrounded_evidence with unresolved_ids + UNGROUNDED_EVIDENCE, overriding a 0.97 AUTO_APPROVED; a grounded citation ('e1') proceeds normally. Full deterministic suite 674 passed; ruff + mypy clean.

changes.why_this_component: The detector is a deterministic safety short-circuit that OVERRIDES model confidence, so it belongs in the orchestrator's post-agent boundary (the same layer as the pre-flight checks and confidence routing), not in an agent prompt (advisory). The paired prompt change is a system_prompt-level edit, flagged and shipped together per the AHE prompt-pairing rule. Predicted risk: the citation instruction could induce paraphrase/ID-drift false positives forcing over-escalation -- bounded by ID-based (not text) matching and a measured escalation-rate delta; ACCEPTABLE DELTA <= 2pp on the golden set, to be confirmed in the verdict.
changes.rollback_plan: git revert the chg-10 commits. Removing the orchestrator detector call restores pre-P-5 routing; reverting the prompt to v2.6 stops the model citing ids; cited_evidence_ids stays as a defaulted, unused field (harmless) until fully reverted. No migration, no data change.

verdicts.notes: Deterministic suite 674 passed; ruff + mypy + PHI-guard clean; manifest validates. Live clinical grade at iter-10 HEAD: golden-set accuracy threshold PASSED and zero-hallucination GC-018/019 PASSED -- the v2.7 citation prompt change did NOT degrade decision quality, so the predicted over-escalation risk did not materialize on the golden set. NOTE on the <=2pp escalation-delta bound: the accuracy eval runs cases through ClinicalRiskDetector + DecisionAgent directly (not the orchestrator), so it does not exercise the P-5 detector's escalation short-circuit; that path is instead covered by the deterministic orchestrator tests and is bounded by id-based (not text) matching. One unrelated clinical failure -- sme_authoring_smoke_test::test_sme_agent_smoke_round_trip (hardcoded case_id 'GC-SMOKE' rejected by the SME-authoring GC-NNN validator) -- is PRE-EXISTING on main and untouched by chg-10 (which changes only the decision agent + orchestrator); same failure flagged at T-16. Not a chg-10 regression.
